LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JAM CITY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JAM CITY, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED IN THEIR ENTIRETY. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) IN ADDITION, THE STAY IS LIFTED AS OF 1/28/19.(AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) COUNSEL FOR LOYTR IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO DEFENDANT JAM CITY, INC.'S MOTION TO STAY ACTION PENDING FINAL RESOLUTION OF RELATED CASE, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. JAM CITY, INC'S MOTION FOR STAY IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT STAYS THIS ACTION UNTIL SEPTEMBER 17, 2018 TO GIVE TINYCO, INC. AN OPPORTUNITY TO PAY THE FINALIZED JUDGMENT IN CASE CGC-16-551519. THE HEARING ON THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT CURRENTLY SET FOR AUGUST 10, 2018 IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 14, 2018. THE TRIAL DATE IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 28, 2019. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT GRANTED DEFENDANT JAM CITY, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO EXTEND STAY OF THIS ACTION PENDING FINAL RESOLUTION OF RELATED CASE AND APPEALS (IN PART) BASED UPON THE AGREEMENTS BY THE PARTIES SET FORTH ON THE RECORD AS FOLLOWS: TO THE EXTENT THAT THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION BROUGHT BY JAM CITY SEEKS ANY RELIEF ON ANY GROUND OTHER THAN RES JUDICATA, THAT MOTION IS NOW OFF CALENDAR AT THE REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY. THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION IS LIMITED TO RES JUDICATA. ALL DISCOVERY IS STAYED UNTIL JAM CITY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DECIDED. THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE OF JANUARY 28TH, 2019 IS CONTINUED TO JULY 29TH, 2019 AND ALL DISCOVERY TRIGGERS OFF THE NEW TRIAL DATE. COUNSEL FOR JAM CITY IS DIRECTED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH THE COURT'S RULING. THE COURT REQUESTED THAT COUNSEL SUBMIT THE ORDER IN THE NEXT COUPLE OF DAYS OR TODAY IF POSSIBLE. (PLEASE SEE THE OFFICIAL COURT REPORTER TRANSCRIPT FOR COMPLETE DETAILS OF THE COURT'S RULING.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. =(302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-12-2019 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2018 Continued To Jul-29-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Jam City, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Micah Jacobs, Esq. 415-445-4696 for Plaintiff.
Robert Simpson, Esq. 831-373-7500 of Kennedy Archer & Griffin for Defendant Jam City, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views re: two main issues - has Jams City's revenue been litigated, did Jam city waive it's right to claim preclusion. Defendant Argues, cites Hunt case. The Court suggests a settlement conference. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Jam City, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Jam City, Inc's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. As an initial matter, the determination of the amount owed by TinyCo per the Amended and Restated Platform Agreement is not final (and thus not subject to claim preclusion) due to plaintiff Loytr, Inc.'s filing of an appeal in case number 551519. Even if the determination of the amount owed by TinyCo to Loytr was final and amenable to claim preclusion, on the record on this motion there are triable disputes: a) whether the parties to case number 551519 actually litigated the issue of the amount, if any, owed by Jam City to Loytr for revenue Jam City received for TinyCo games covered by the Agreement; and b) if the parties to case number 551519 did not actually litigate that issue, whether Jam City waived its right to assert the defense of claim preclusion due to its conduct in case number 551519 and this case. Loytr's claims against Jam City are based on the same primary right as its claims against TinyCo adjudicated in 551519 because in both actions Loytr seeks to recover monies alleged owed to it under the Agreement. It is far from clear based on the record on this motion whether the issue of the amount, if any, owed by Jam City to Loytr for revenue Jam City received for TinyCo games covered by the Agreement was actually litigated in case number 551519 due to the parties' failure to provide definitive evidence of what actually occurred at the trial in that case and the parties' disagreements about what actually occurred at the trial. Significantly, as the party who bears the initial burden on all issues on this motion, Jam City did not submit any evidence in its moving papers whether the issue of Jam City's receipt of any revenue from TinyCo Games (apart from the revenue received by TinyCo that was actually litigated) that was subject to revenue share under the Agreement was actually litigated. Per United Bank & Trust Co. of California v. Hunt (1934) 1 Cal.2d 340, there is a triable issue whether Jam City waived its right to object to the splitting of claims based on among, other things, the parties' stipulation regarding the motion to consolidate the two cases. The penultimate sentence in Gamble v. General Foods Corporation (1991) 229 Cal. App. 3d 893 distinguishing United Bank does not require granting this motion because Loyter's waiver argument is not based solely or even mostly on the denial of its motion to amend in case 551519.

In addition, the stay is lifted as of 1/28/19.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for Loytr is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Extend Stay
Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Micah R. Jacobs, Esq. of Jacobs Law Group SF, 415.445.4696, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Crystal Mothershead Gaudette, Esq. of Kennedy Archer & Giffen, 831.373.7500, appearing for the Defendant.

DEFENDANT JAM CITY, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO EXTEND STAY OF THIS ACTION PENDING FINAL RESOLUTION OF RELATED CASE AND APPEALS.

Matter called, ARGUMENT held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court Granted the motion (in part) based upon the agreements by the parties set forth on the record as follows: to the extent that the motion for summary judgment motion brought by Jam City seeks any relief on any ground other than res judicata, that motion is now off calendar at the request of the moving party. The summary judgment motion is limited to res judicata. All discovery is stayed until Jam City's motion for summary judgment is decided. The current trial date of January 28th, 2019 is continued to July 29th, 2019 and all discovery triggers off the new trial date. Counsel for JAM City is directed to prepare a proposed form of order that complies with the Court's ruling. The Court requested that counsel submit the order in the next couple of days or today if possible. (Please see the official court reporter transcript for complete details of the Court's ruling.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

Off Calendar Dec-22-2017 Continued To Feb-28-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Micah R. Jacobs of the law offices of Jacobs Law Group SF (415-445-4696) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Phil K. Rucker of the law offices of Kennedy Archer & Giffen (831-373-7500) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party, Jam City, Inc.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT JAM CITY, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the entirety of the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Loytr, Inc. is DENIED. The first amended complaint adequately alleges that TinyCo, Inc. assigned its obligations under the Revenue Share Agreement to Jam City. Loytr's failure to file the first amended complaint in the time frame required by the July 20, 2017 order is not a basis to preclude it from filing the first amended complaint at least where, as here, Jam City did not seek entry of judgment prior to Loytr's filing of the first amended complaint and Jam City has not shown that it was prejudiced by Loytr's delay. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org (L&M302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Micah R. Jacobs of the law offices of Jacobs Law Group SF (415-445-4696) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Phil K. Rucker of the law offices of Kennedy Archer & Giffen (831-373-7500) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party, Jam City, Inc.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT JAM CITY, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to both causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Loytr, Inc. is OVERRULED as to both causes of action. The first amended complaint adequately alleges that TinyCo, Inc. assigned its obligations under the Revenue Share Agreement to Jam City. To the extent that there are Jam City games that are not Tinyco games, those Jam City games are not covered by the agreement. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org (L&M302)
